steps.title: Locate the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is where the graph crosses the y-axis. Every point on the y-axis has $x=0$.

steps.title: Translate to function notation
steps.content: The value $h(0)$ is the function's output when $x=0$. Since the y-coordinate of that intercept is $k$, $h(0)$ must equal $k$. The answer is A.
